Recent updates in NYC include a man allegedly setting fires in his home to kill spiders, the closure of restaurants due to health orders, and a significant slowdown in PATH train commutes. Additionally, potholes are costing drivers thousands, and the NYPD continues to face scrutiny over illegal stops.

### Risks & Alerts

- Man allegedly set fires in home to kill spiders, left U-Haul in NYC sparking bomb squad scare. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMi_?oc=5)
- NYC restaurants ordered closed from February 20 to 27 due to health concerns. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Rush hour commute slowed by suspended PATH train service from NYC to Hoboken. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMirAFBVV95cUxPSk50NlVacHkzdG1SUWhPajAtRU5TcnBlYkY4cTA0OHJTZzJJdGZhM0M4RmRMQmZLYnVtbERyQVYtV3lpa3lVb1VIZVV0a3o4WEktdTBMVjZHdmxlXzZGMnlhVVJBTkc4cnNVUmpaS2MwakZzX2JjbDduQjBia1E1LWNXY0RSMDB3aDRVdlhyalBtT1d6dXVPRlg0a2RTY056b0ZMd3kzQjB3RmtL?oc=5)
- Potholes causing significant repair costs for drivers, impacting daily commutes. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiiwFBVV95cUxOYXcwaXpMbjZfbmpHMzZCZ3dxUEQ2aGNsVlVmR1JOYTVlcFdjNi1GSWtwenVRbjdsSlgwTFJTalRRTDNVOE03MVhvZk1GT2VhZ1ZmeEZveU9fOF83SGtNRG85QzJkTUxBdUt6NXZJNGxKRE12MG5Pc3BFS0VCM1JoREJHR0IzWC1DWGFJ?oc=5)

### Civics & Politics

- NYPD continues to illegally stop New Yorkers, violating a court ruling, according to a monitor's report. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMidkFVX3lxTE5mcUF3czAwRnNlRFl1ZVhPcVR4akRIMG50aVB4NFU5alIxQWV0NmFIYXZMR1BicURWSm9DdnVWc214bnp1c213d1N0WFNTRmtESHNSV05TaDhyTFhIYW1zaDNXMlE3TWx4TXZBSlhaVUk5eVFTOEE?oc=5)
- City officials warn that immigration enforcement may be impersonating housing inspectors, urging residents to verify identities. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiiAFBVV95cUxQNlVGbXM4dHYxRFowNmwtMEhIV1k1aU51bmVxTG1ZRUt6T3dNT3QzY2Q5c3MyenVGLS05aEtFV0h3cnk1SVFNclhJaWRMMnlOdmhTSlczQnU0OEx4bXhWbl9qMmI5OWREOUJ5cUhncUpGNnVuckhlUnBUS2k1YU52b3FqbEVPVXZm?oc=5)
- Climate law mandates could increase energy bills for New Yorkers by $4,000, according to state analysis. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Brooklyn Public Library has ended passport services following an order from the State Department. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMigwFBVV95cUxQS2NDeVRINXBxRFBFeWFza0t5enRmbEFVWnB2S3R6UmYxLTNCNzc3VHBMVGFFLUF1U2J5WUdfUkg3a0VUM3BtZjhYSzg0SzFNSU1NWXRFbzdzNDFfVGJ0MnhtRER5MEhxYUp4cVRQc3FheHBnWlFERDE3UnlseDlGTTQzWQ?oc=5)
- Founder of a real estate development firm in the South Bronx pleads guilty to fraud charges. - Mamdani's proposed budget cuts could reduce funding for NYC parks previously labeled as 'neglected.' ### Opportunities & Welfare

- SNAP work requirements take effect March 1; eligible New Yorkers must comply to maintain benefits. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMickFVX3lxTFBJclFYUU5TRV9haU5wQ24xcDAzTUhwRzFaOHFUamJJN3VFN0JOclFLT0dvc3NlcXR2SEJsbTRDTW13LUFzcXdPcUJtTHlnbS1mclFQbU5XeXlvYzlMY3FfZkNGLS1uZlRZdnl4cmd1Vk92dw?oc=5)
